name: The Global Heat Flow Database: Release 2024 (GHFDB v2024, version 2026.03)
provider: International Heat Flow Commission; GFZ Data Services
theme_canonical: Global point database
theme_raw: global heat-flow measurements and quality-assessed metadata
coverage: global
license: CC BY 4.0 / GFZ Data Services terms
formats: XLSX, TXT/CSV, shapefile via heatflow.world, metadata PDF
access: DOI landing page / downloadable files / heatflow.world portal
time_span: measurements/data generated 1939-2024; release 2024, revised 2026.03
scale: 91,182 heat-flow data points; point measurements
source_type: official global database release
evidence: GFZ/IHFC describe the release as 91,182 heat-flow data from 1,586 publications, with continental and oceanic domains and quality-code reassessment.
notes: Canonical current global heat-flow point database. Supersedes 2023 and 2021 GFZ releases for most use, but older releases are useful for provenance/history.
desc_zh: IHFC/GFZ 全球热流数据库 2024 版是当前全球地热热流点数据的核心开放来源，2026.03 版本继续修订质量代码和元数据。数据覆盖陆地与海洋域，汇集 1939-2024 年间来自 1,586 篇文献的 91,182 条热流记录。适合作为全球热流统计、质量筛选、区域子集抽取和地热背景场分析的主干数据源。
desc_en: The IHFC/GFZ Global Heat Flow Database release 2024, version 2026.03, is the current canonical open compilation of global terrestrial and marine heat-flow measurements. It aggregates 91,182 records from 1,586 publications spanning data generated between 1939 and 2024, with metadata and quality-code reassessment. It is the backbone source for global heat-flow statistics, regional subsetting, quality screening, and geothermal background studies.

openness: open-data-with-conditions
access_class: open

provider: GFZ Data Services; Petrunin, Neumann, Norden, Balkan-Pazvantoğlu, Elbarbary, Elger, Jennings, Fuchs
theme_canonical: Global gridded product
theme_raw: quality-assessed measurements, gridded products, kriging interpolation
coverage: global
license: GFZ Data Services data publication terms
formats: data files and Jupyter notebook via GFZ Data Services
access: DOI landing page / downloadable research data
time_span: published 2026; derived from GHFDB v2024
scale: gridded products and interpolation derived from GHFDB v2024
source_type: official derived global product
evidence: ESSD 2026 data availability states that quality-assessed measurements, gridded products, kriging interpolation and a Jupyter notebook are available via this GFZ DOI.
notes: Newly surfaced current lead as of July 2026. Treat as a derived product from GHFDB v2024, not a replacement for the point database.
desc_zh: 该 GFZ 数据出版物提供基于 IHFC 2024 全球热流数据库的质量评估测点、网格化产品、克里金插值结果和用于提取/可视化的 Jupyter Notebook。它把点状热流记录转化为更便于建模和制图的格网表达。适合需要连续热流场、插值不确定性或复现实验图件的研究。
desc_en: This GFZ data publication provides quality-assessed GHFDB v2024 measurements, gridded products, kriging interpolation outputs, and a Jupyter notebook used to extract and visualize the data. It complements the point database by providing analysis-ready gridded representations of global heat flow. It is useful for modelling workflows that need continuous heat-flow fields, interpolation products, or reproducible figures.

openness: open-data-with-conditions

name: The Global Heat Flow Database of the IHFC, University of North Dakota copy (PANGAEA 2012)
provider: International Heat Flow Commission; University of North Dakota; PANGAEA

theme_raw: component parts of World Heat Flow Data Collection
coverage: global
formats: tab-delimited text, HTML preview, citation metadata
access: PANGAEA DOI landing page / direct tab-delimited download
time_span: data copied 2012-10; source records older historical compilations
scale: 82,442 data points in PANGAEA record
source_type: historical repository dataset
evidence: PANGAEA lists 82,442 data points, heat-flow parameter fields, CC-BY-3.0 license, and tab-delimited download for the 2012 IHFC/UND copy.
notes: Historical baseline with incomplete references and uncertain-origin subsets. Use mainly for provenance comparison against modern GFZ/IHFC releases.
desc_zh: PANGAEA 上的 2012 年 IHFC/UND 全球热流数据库副本保存了现代 GFZ 发布体系之前的一版历史全球编译。页面提供 CC BY 3.0 许可、82,442 条数据点、字段说明和制表符文本下载。由于其说明中标出部分记录来源不完整或不确定，适合做版本追溯和历史对比，而不宜替代 2024/2026 质量评估版。
desc_en: The PANGAEA 2012 IHFC/University of North Dakota copy preserves an older global heat-flow compilation from before the modern GFZ release workflow. The record provides CC BY 3.0 licensing, 82,442 data points, field definitions, and tab-delimited downloads. Because PANGAEA notes uncertain or incomplete provenance for parts of the compilation, it is best used for historical comparison and provenance audits rather than as the preferred current database.

provider: GFZ Data Services; Fuchs, Norden and Förster
theme_canonical: National point database
theme_raw: onshore Germany heat-flow determinations
coverage: Germany onshore
license: CC BY 4.0 / GFZ Data Services terms
formats: downloadable data and description via GFZ Data Services
access: DOI landing page / downloadable data package
scale: 836 determinations at 595 locations
source_type: national database release
evidence: GFZ page states the database contains all onshore Germany heat-flow data, 836 determinations at 595 locations from 42 publications, generated 1959-2020.
notes: Included as a national update that is explicitly cited as a predecessor/part of current GHFDB workflows.
desc_zh: 德国热流数据库 2022 是 GFZ 发布的德国陆上热流数据集，覆盖 1959-2020 年间的测定结果。数据包括 42 篇文献中的 595 个位置、836 个热流测定值，并补充了 2002 年地热图集之后的新测定和遗漏旧资料。它适合德国区域地热背景场、井孔热流和全国热流编译对比研究。
desc_en: The German Heat Flow Database 2022 is a GFZ national release for onshore Germany. It covers heat-flow determinations generated between 1959 and 2020, including 836 values at 595 locations from 42 publications and adding both post-2002 measurements and older records missing from previous atlas compilations. It is a strong source for German geothermal background, borehole heat-flow analysis, and comparison with the global compilation.

openness: open-data-with-conditions
access_class: open
name: Update of the Heat Flow Database in Mexico and surrounding areas
provider: GFZ Data Services; Fuentes-Bustillos, Neumann, Norden and Fuchs
theme_canonical: National/regional point database
theme_raw: Mexico and surrounding seas heat-flow database
coverage: Mexico and surrounding seas
formats: downloadable data and data description via GFZ Data Services
access: DOI landing page / downloadable data package
scale: 1,230 heat-flow determinations from 25 publications
source_type: national/regional database release
evidence: GFZ metadata states 1,230 heat-flow determinations from 25 publications, 49% onshore and 51% offshore, with data covering 1970-2022 and CC BY 4.0 rights.
notes: One of the explicit HasPart components of the 2024 GHFDB release; keep separate because it has regional curation and DOI.
desc_zh: 墨西哥及周边海域热流数据库更新整合了全球热流评估项目目录中与墨西哥陆域和邻近海域相关的研究。GFZ 元数据记录其包含 25 篇文献中的 1,230 个热流测定，约一半为陆上数据、一半为海上数据，时间覆盖 1970-2022 年。该数据集有独立 DOI 和 CC BY 4.0 许可，适合墨西哥及加利福尼亚湾邻区热流研究。
desc_en: This GFZ release updates heat-flow data for Mexico and surrounding seas using literature collected through the Global Heat Flow Data Assessment Project. It contains 1,230 heat-flow determinations from 25 publications, split between onshore and offshore records, and covers 1970-2022. With its own DOI and CC BY 4.0 licensing, it is a curated regional source for Mexico and adjacent marine geothermal studies.

openness: open-data-with-conditions

notes: Need next pass to extract exact row count/time span from the GFZ data description; accepted because DOI, provider, relevance and access route are clear.
desc_zh: 印度热流数据库更新是 GFZ Data Services 发布的国家级热流数据集，也是 IHFC/GHFDB 2024 版引用的组成部分之一。它按全球热流数据库的新结构整理印度区域热流记录，并提供独立 DOI 作为下载和引用入口。后续应进一步读取数据说明以补齐精确记录数、时间范围和文件结构。
desc_en: The India Heat Flow Database update is a GFZ Data Services national dataset and an explicit component cited by the GHFDB 2024 release. It organizes Indian heat-flow records using the modern Global Heat Flow Database structure and provides an independent DOI for access and citation. A follow-up pass should extract the exact record count, time span, and file inventory from the data description.

source_type: national database release

notes: One of the explicit HasPart components of GHFDB v2024. The GFZ PDF snippet has a title typo mentioning Mexico; use DOI/landing metadata as canonical.
desc_zh: Türkiye 热流数据库更新是 GFZ Data Services 发布的土耳其国家级热流编译，并作为 GHFDB 2024 的组成数据集出现。元数据说明其包含 1991-2023 年间 9 篇文献中的 725 个热流测定，并遵循 IHFC/GHFDB 新结构。该数据集适合安纳托利亚及邻近构造带地热背景和区域热流对比。
desc_en: The Türkiye Heat Flow Database update is a GFZ Data Services national compilation and an explicit component of GHFDB v2024. The metadata reports 725 heat-flow determinations from 9 publications generated between 1991 and 2023, structured according to the IHFC/GHFDB schema. It is useful for geothermal background and regional heat-flow studies in Anatolia and surrounding tectonic settings.

provider: GFZ Data Services; Neumann, Negrete-Aranda and Contreras

access: DOI landing page / downloadable data package
time_span: published 2023; measurement time span source-dependent
scale: regional offshore heat-flow measurements
source_type: marine regional dataset
evidence: GFZpublic and ESSD relations identify this as a GFZ Data Services dataset containing all offshore heat-flow data in the Guaymas Basin.
notes: Important high-heat-flow marine rift-basin dataset; separate from the broader Mexico update because it has its own DOI and focused offshore scope.
desc_zh: Guaymas Basin 热流数据集是 GFZ Data Services 发布的加利福尼亚湾海上热流区域数据。该 DOI 被 2026 年 GHFDB 论文引用为独立数据集，说明其包含 Guaymas Basin 海上热流数据。它适合研究年轻裂谷、热液循环、海底沉积盆地热输运和墨西哥区域热流异常。
desc_en: The Guaymas Basin heat-flow dataset is a GFZ Data Services regional marine data publication for the Gulf of California. It is cited by the 2026 GHFDB paper as an independent dataset containing offshore heat-flow data from the Guaymas Basin. It is valuable for studies of young rifts, hydrothermal circulation, marine sedimentary basins, and regional heat-flow anomalies around Mexico.

provider: GEUS Dataverse; Colgan, Wansing et al.
theme_canonical: Polar point and gridded database

coverage: Greenland and within 500 km of coastline
formats: NetCDF, XYZ, XLSX, shapefile ZIP, metadata exports
access: Dataverse landing page / public file downloads
doi_or_citation: 10.22008/FK2/F9P03L; related ESSD 2022 Greenland Geothermal Heat Flow Database and Map
time_span: published 2021/2022; updated as new measurements become available
scale: 10 km interpolated map; 55 km native map; point measurement database
source_type: polar regional database and model
evidence: GEUS Dataverse lists public NetCDF maps, XYZ outputs, an XLSX measurement database and shapefile downloads, with description as all geothermal heat-flow measurements within 500 km of Greenland coastline.
notes: Strong polar-source row with both measurements and machine-learning map. Include NGRIP variants as files, not separate rows.
desc_zh: Greenland Geothermal Heat Flow Database and Map 汇集格陵兰海岸 500 km 范围内的地热热流测量，并给出机器学习热流图。GEUS Dataverse 提供 NetCDF 格网、XYZ 输出、测量数据库 XLSX、测点 shapefile 和热泉清单等公开文件。它是冰盖底边界、冰流动力学和北极地热背景研究的重要开放数据源。
desc_en: The Greenland Geothermal Heat Flow Database and Map compiles geothermal heat-flow measurements within 500 km of Greenland’s coastline and provides machine-learning heat-flow maps. GEUS Dataverse exposes public NetCDF grids, XYZ outputs, an XLSX measurement database, measurement shapefiles, and related hot-spring inventories. It is a key open source for ice-sheet basal-boundary studies, ice dynamics, and Arctic geothermal background analysis.

openness: open-data-with-conditions

name: Antarctic geothermal heat flow model: Aq1
provider: PANGAEA; Stål, Reading, Halpin and Whittaker
theme_canonical: Polar gridded model
theme_raw: Antarctic geothermal heat-flow model Aq1
coverage: Antarctica
formats: PANGAEA downloadable dataset
access: PANGAEA DOI landing page / data download
doi_or_citation: 10.1594/PANGAEA.924857; related article 10.1029/2020GC009428
time_span: published 2020; model derived from multi-observable predictors
scale: Antarctic heat-flow model, native resolution described in associated work as about 20 km
source_type: polar gridded model
evidence: PANGAEA page describes Aq1 as a refined geothermal heat-flow map for Antarctica generated from multiple observables and trained with 5,792 high-quality heat-flow values from other continents.
notes: Keep as canonical Aq1 record; regridded/ISMIP7 packages should reference it as derived products.
desc_zh: Aq1 是 PANGAEA 发布的南极地热热流模型，用多种地球物理和地质观测量通过相似性检测方法生成。数据说明指出模型利用来自其他大陆的 5,792 个高质量热流值，并结合南极的全球、洲际和区域观测量。它是南极冰盖底部热边界条件、构造解释和极地热流不确定性分析中的重要开放模型。
desc_en: Aq1 is a PANGAEA-published Antarctic geothermal heat-flow model generated with a similarity-detection approach using multiple geophysical and geological observables. The dataset description states that it attributes Antarctic observables to 5,792 high-quality heat-flow values from other continents. It is an important open model for Antarctic ice-sheet basal boundary conditions, tectonic interpretation, and polar heat-flow uncertainty analysis.

provider: Zenodo; Lösing et al.
theme_canonical: Polar derived gridded product
theme_raw: regridded and topographically corrected geothermal heat-flow fields
coverage: Greenland and Antarctica
license: Zenodo open dataset terms / cite original datasets
formats: ZIP archives with gridded and corrected data
access: Zenodo DOI landing page / downloadable ZIP files
time_span: published 2025; derived from existing Greenland and Antarctic heat-flow datasets
scale: multi-GB gridded products; files include 1.5 GB, 1.9 GB and 2.0 GB archives
source_type: derived polar modelling dataset
evidence: Zenodo record lists GHF_Regridded.zip, GHF_Topographically_Corrected.zip, Original_Data.zip and Topographic_Correction.zip with DOI 10.5281/zenodo.17745730.
notes: Accepted as open downloadable derived data. A related Zenodo record 17051524 is marked restricted and is deferred until access terms are clarified.
desc_zh: 该 Zenodo 数据集为 ISMIP7 相关研究提供格陵兰和南极地热热流的再网格化与地形校正版本。记录列出多个可下载 ZIP，包括再网格化热流、地形校正热流、原始数据和地形校正流程文件。它适合冰盖模式统一边界条件、不同热流产品比较和地形效应敏感性分析。
desc_en: This Zenodo dataset provides regridded and topographically corrected geothermal heat-flow products for Greenland and Antarctica as an ISMIP7-related supplement. The record lists downloadable ZIP archives for regridded heat flow, topographically corrected heat flow, original data, and topographic correction workflows. It is useful for harmonizing ice-sheet model boundary conditions, comparing heat-flow products, and testing topographic correction effects.

openness: open-data-with-conditions

theme_canonical: National well and heat-flow archive
theme_raw: US heat flow, temperature gradient, BHT and well data
coverage: United States
formats: ZIP archives, Excel index files, state/institution file packages
access: GDR landing page / downloadable archives
doi_or_citation: GDR_Dataset_1704; Blackwell, Richards and Chickering Pace (2014)
time_span: assembled before 2014; moved to GDR for open access in 2026
scale: over 44,000 wells and over 6,000 related geothermal exploration resources
source_type: national geothermal archive
evidence: GDR page states the collection compiles heat flow and temperature-gradient data from over 44,000 wells plus more than 6,000 related resources, with public access and CC license.
notes: Major U.S. open archive. Includes equilibrium heat-flow database, corrected BHT wells, indexes, reports and supporting documents; not all files are heat-flow measurements.
desc_zh: SMU/NGDS 全国热流与井资料归档是美国地热开放资料的重要集合，2026 年迁移到 GDR 开放访问。页面说明其包含超过 44,000 口井的热流、温度梯度和相关资料，以及 6,000 多个地热勘查资源，并以 ZIP 包和 Excel 索引组织。它适合美国区域热流、井温、BHT 校正和地热资源评价研究。
desc_en: The SMU/NGDS nationwide heat-flow and well-data archive is a major U.S. geothermal open-data collection, moved to GDR for open access in 2026. The landing page reports heat-flow and temperature-gradient data from more than 44,000 wells plus over 6,000 related geothermal exploration resources, organized as ZIP archives with Excel indexes. It supports U.S. regional heat-flow, well-temperature, corrected BHT, and geothermal resource assessment workflows.

openness: open-data-with-conditions

name: USGS Contributions to the Nevada Geothermal Machine Learning Project: Heat Flow Data
provider: U.S. Geological Survey
theme_canonical: Regional heat-flow raster and wells
theme_raw: Nevada conductive heat-flow map surface and well data
coverage: northern Nevada, USA
formats: USGS ScienceBase/data release files via DOI; XML metadata
access: DOI landing page / digital data download
time_span: published/updated 2021
scale: regional map surface and well data; spatial extent -119.5306,38.4480,-114.4170,40.7282
source_type: regional USGS data release
evidence: Data.gov/USGS page describes a conductive heat-flow map surface in northern Nevada generated from wells with measured thermal gradient and thermal conductivity, plus well data and standard-error surface.
notes: Focused machine-learning geothermal play-fairway input; overlaps conceptually with SMU national database but is a distinct USGS DOI product.
desc_zh: 该 USGS 数据发布为内华达北部地热机器学习项目提供导热热流空间变化图面和井资料。数据说明指出图面由具有热梯度和热导率估计的井点生成，主要使用 SMU 数据并加入部分 USGS 数据，同时包含插值标准误差图面。它适合地热远景区机器学习、热流插值和区域资源评价。
desc_en: This USGS data release provides a conductive heat-flow surface and well data for the northern Nevada Geothermal Machine Learning Project. The documentation says the surface was generated from wells with estimated heat flow based on measured thermal gradient and thermal conductivity, mainly using SMU data plus additional USGS data, and includes an interpolation standard-error surface. It is useful for geothermal play-fairway machine learning, heat-flow interpolation, and regional resource assessment.

openness: open-data-with-conditions

name: USGS T-3 Original Thermal Gradient, Thermal Conductivity, and Heat Flow Data from T-3 Ice Island, 1963-73
provider: U.S. Geological Survey
theme_canonical: Marine Arctic point data
theme_raw: T-3 Ice Island marine thermal gradient, conductivity and heat-flow data
coverage: Amerasian Basin, Arctic Ocean
formats: USGS data release files via DOI; XML metadata
access: DOI landing page / digital data download
time_span: 1963-1973; published digital release 2020
scale: 584 attempted penetrations; data reported at 356 sites
source_type: marine field data release
evidence: Data.gov/USGS states the T-3 Ice Island study acquired marine heat-flow data and coincident cores in the Arctic Ocean; at least 584 penetrations were attempted and data were reported at 356 sites.
notes: Original table retains non-SI units; useful for Arctic marine heat-flow provenance and reassessment.
desc_zh: USGS T-3 Ice Island 数据集数字化了 1963-1973 年漂流冰岛科考期间的北冰洋热流观测。资料覆盖 Canada Basin、Nautilus Basin、Mendeleev Ridge 和 Alpha Ridge 等区域，至少尝试 584 次热流探针贯入，并在 356 个站点报告数据。它是北极海洋热流、热导率和温度梯度历史资料的重要开放来源。
desc_en: The USGS T-3 Ice Island release digitizes original Arctic Ocean heat-flow observations from the 1963-1973 drifting ice-island program. It covers areas including Canada Basin, Nautilus Basin, Mendeleev Ridge, and Alpha Ridge, with at least 584 attempted heat-flow penetrations and reported data at 356 sites. It is an important open source for historical Arctic marine heat flow, thermal conductivity, and thermal-gradient reassessment.

coverage: Utah, USA

access: UGS NGDS landing page / linked downloads

source_type: state agency data portal
evidence: UGS page lists Heat Flow in Utah resources including GIS files for heat-flow map, metadata spreadsheet, about 500 new/existing heat-flow determinations, and more than 2,300 Utah rock thermal-conductivity measurements.
notes: Portal row rather than a single DOI; next pass should resolve stable direct file URLs for GIS and metadata spreadsheet.
desc_zh: Utah Geological Survey 的 NGDS 页面集中提供犹他州热流和地热相关资料。页面说明热流部分包括由约 500 个新旧热流测定生成的背景热流图、全州连续地表温度资料，以及 2,300 多个岩石热导率测量组成的数据库，并给出 GIS 文件和元数据表链接。它适合州级地热资源评价和热流图复核。
desc_en: The Utah Geological Survey NGDS page centralizes Utah heat-flow and geothermal datasets. Its heat-flow section describes a background heat-flow map generated from about 500 new and existing determinations, continuous surface ground-temperature information, and a thermal-conductivity database with more than 2,300 Utah rock measurements, with GIS files and metadata spreadsheets linked. It is useful for state-level geothermal resource assessment and heat-flow map review.

notes: Accepted as institutional access route. Next pass should mine GeoNetwork records for individual heat-flow layers and licenses.
desc_zh: UK Geothermal Platform 是由 BGS 维护的英国地热数据平台，2025 年首版整合多个机构的地热、地质、水文、井孔和地震资料。页面说明平台有 90 多个图层，包含热流和温度图，并提供地图浏览器与元数据目录用于检索、浏览和下载。它是英国热流图、深浅层地热潜力和遗留地热数据的机构级入口。
desc_en: The UK Geothermal Platform is a BGS-maintained national geothermal data platform whose first 2025 release integrates data from multiple UK organizations. The site states that it provides more than 90 layers, including heat-flow and temperature maps, and offers a map explorer plus a metadata catalogue for search, browsing, and downloads. It is the institutional gateway for UK heat-flow maps, shallow and deep geothermal potential layers, and legacy geothermal datasets.

desc_zh: Davies 2013 全球固体地球表面热流图是在 2° × 2° 等面积格网上构建的全球热流图件，IHFC mapping 页面说明其基于 38,000 多个热流测量，并结合年轻洋壳冷却模型与有数据区域的观测平均。该图是全球热损失、热流背景场和低分辨率边界条件研究的经典文献产品。后续需要进一步固定 Google Earth 文件的直接下载链接和许可。
desc_en: The Davies 2013 global solid-Earth surface heat-flow map is a 2° by 2° equal-area gridded product. The IHFC mapping page describes it as based on more than 38,000 measurements, combining young-ocean-crust cooling-model estimates with observed averages in measured regions. It remains a classic literature-backed product for global heat-loss estimates, background heat-flow fields, and low-resolution boundary-condition studies; a follow-up pass should pin down the direct Google Earth file URL and license.

desc_en: This GFZ Data Services publication supports the Haeger, Petrunin, and Kaban Antarctic geothermal heat-flow and lithospheric thermal-structure study. The associated paper presents a three-dimensional thermal and heat-flow model for Antarctica, relevant to tectonic interpretation, volcanic regions, and ice-dynamics boundary conditions. It complements Aq1, Martos 2017, and other Antarctic geothermal heat-flow model families.

desc_en: The Martos et al. 2017 PANGAEA dataset provides Antarctic geothermal heat-flux distribution, estimated Curie depths, and links to gridded files. The record describes a 15 km high-resolution heat-flux map and uncertainty distribution derived from spectral analysis of airborne magnetic compilations. It is a canonical open product for Antarctic ice-sheet basal boundary conditions and multi-model geothermal heat-flow comparisons.

desc_en: The Ridge B dataset focuses on local Antarctic subglacial bed condition and geothermal heat-flow inference. The Zenodo record explicitly identifies bed condition and geothermal heat flow in the Ridge B region, making it relevant to East Antarctic local heat-flow inversion, subglacial hydrology, and old-ice preservation studies. A follow-up pass should extract the file inventory, variables, and detailed license information.

desc_zh: Totten Glacier Thermal Structure 是东南极 Totten Glacier 区域热结构研究的 Zenodo 数据集，并被后续评价八种地热热流图的研究引用。它可作为局地冰盖热结构、滑动条件和不同热流边界产品一致性检验的资料入口。后续应解析 Zenodo 文件清单，确认其中热流/温度/模型输出变量。
desc_en: Totten Glacier Thermal Structure is a Zenodo dataset for regional thermal-structure work at Totten Glacier in East Antarctica and is cited by later studies evaluating geothermal heat-flow maps in the region. It is a useful entry point for local ice-sheet thermal structure, sliding-condition analysis, and consistency tests among geothermal heat-flow boundary products. A follow-up pass should parse the Zenodo file list and confirm heat-flow, temperature, and model-output variables.

notes: Not a primary heat-flow compilation; accepted as a derived data package that operationalizes multiple Greenland GHF estimates for proxy assessment.
desc_zh: 该 Zenodo 开放数据集为利用稳定同位素评估格陵兰地热热流的研究提供补充数据。页面说明它包含七种 GHF 估计方案对应的模型输出，文件包括 TXT 和 NPY 格式的基底水文势、基底融化率、冰龄、同位素和水通量等变量。它不是原始热流测点库，而是将多个格陵兰热流估计用于冰盖水文/同位素代理检验的派生数据包。
desc_en: This open Zenodo dataset supports a study using stable isotopes as a proxy for assessing Greenland geothermal heat flow. The record states that it contains products for seven GHF estimates, with TXT and NPY outputs for basal hydropotential, melt rate, basal ice age, isotopic composition, and basal water flux. It is not a primary heat-flow measurement database, but it operationalizes multiple Greenland GHF estimates for ice-sheet hydrology and isotope-proxy assessment.

desc_zh: Hazzard 和 Richards 的 OSF 数据集支持其 2024 年基于地震学资料反演南极地热热流、地壳热导率和放射性生热的研究。论文资料可用性部分指向 OSF 项目，说明该数据集提供南极热流与地壳热参数推断结果。它适合与磁法、机器学习和岩石圈热结构方法得到的南极热流图进行交叉比较。
desc_en: The Hazzard and Richards OSF dataset supports their 2024 seismology-based inference of Antarctic geothermal heat flow, crustal conductivity, and radiogenic heat production. The article data reference points to the OSF project as the repository for the dataset. It is useful for comparing Antarctic geothermal heat-flow maps derived from seismic, magnetic, machine-learning, and lithospheric thermal-structure approaches.

notes: Accepted as an Australian heat-flow-adjacent source because it exposes the well-temperature and temperature-gradient observations used in heat-flow estimation rather than only a report landing page.
desc_zh: OZTemp 是 Geoscience Australia 通过 Research Data Australia 发布的全国井温与温度梯度开放数据入口，来源于更新后的 AUSTHERM05。记录说明它包含 5,513 口井、17,247 条温度或温度梯度记录，并汇集了已发表热流资料、公司井温记录和 GA 温度测井等来源。它不是单纯的热流图，而是澳大利亚热流估算和地热温度场建模的重要观测底座。
desc_en: OZTemp is Geoscience Australia's open national well-temperature and temperature-gradient data entry point, exposed through Research Data Australia from the updated AUSTHERM05 database. The record states that it contains 5,513 wells and 17,247 temperature and/or gradient records assembled from published heat-flow data, company well temperatures, and Geoscience Australia temperature logs. It is not just a heat-flow map; it is a key observational base for Australian heat-flow estimation and geothermal temperature-field modelling.

desc_zh: Stanford Thermal Earth Model 是覆盖美国本土的开放热结构模型数据集，GDR 页面说明其预测地下温度、地表热流和岩石热导率。资源包括原始井底温度数据、输入输出 CSV、API 和 ArcGIS 图层，适合与 SMU/NGDS、USGS 区域热流点数据进行交叉使用。它属于派生模型产品，但明确提供 surface heat flow 预测，是美国尺度热流信息的重要开放入口。
desc_en: The Stanford Thermal Earth Model is an open conterminous-U.S. thermal-structure dataset whose GDR record states that it predicts subsurface temperature, surface heat flow, and rock thermal conductivity. Resources include raw bottom-hole temperature data, input/output CSV files, an API, and ArcGIS layers, making it useful alongside SMU/NGDS and USGS regional heat-flow data. It is a derived model product, but it explicitly exposes surface heat-flow predictions as an open national-scale access point.

desc_zh: INGENIOUS Great Basin Regional Dataset Compilation 是 GDR 中的大盆地区域地热资料汇编，页面列出 Heat Flow Maps、热导率资料、井/泉温度与化学数据等资源。其热流图说明旨在从背景导热热流预测中剔除热液对流影响，因此对区分区域背景热流与地热异常很有价值。该条目作为区域资料包收录，后续还需要逐文件解析栅格、表格和元数据。
desc_en: The INGENIOUS Great Basin Regional Dataset Compilation is a GDR regional geothermal data package that lists heat-flow maps, thermal-conductivity data, and well/spring temperature and chemistry resources. Its heat-flow-map description says the maps were designed to remove hydrothermal convective influence from background conductive heat-flow predictions, making the package useful for separating regional heat flow from geothermal anomalies. It is accepted as a regional data package, with file-level raster and table metadata still to be unpacked.

desc_en: The Snake River Plain Shallow EGS dataset exposes regional shallow enhanced-geothermal-system resource-potential and mapping data through GDR. The record states that the modelling used well data, a thermal-conductivity model, groundwater-flow considerations, and radioactivity, with keywords including heat flow, thermal conductivity, BHT, well data, and borehole; it provides a final data workbook. It is not a pure heat-flow measurement database, but it is an open regional geothermal-resource package with heat-flow-related inputs and derived map products.

evidence: PANGAEA states that the authors predicted Antarctic geothermal heat flow using machine learning; the record presents a new map ranging from 35 to 156 mW/m² and includes minimum, maximum and difference products for uncertainty assessment, with CC BY 4.0 license.
notes: Complements the previously accepted Aq1, Martos, GFZ lithosphere and OSF seismological Antarctic products; useful for model-family comparison.
desc_zh: Lösing 和 Ebbing 的 PANGAEA 数据集提供基于机器学习的南极地热热流预测图及不确定性产品。记录说明该方法使用多种地球物理和地质数据，通过梯度提升回归树预测 GHF，热流范围约 35-156 mW/m²，并包含最小值、最大值和差异图以评估不确定性。它适合与 Aq1、Martos、GFZ 岩石圈模型和地震学反演产品进行南极热流模型族对比。
desc_en: The Lösing and Ebbing PANGAEA dataset provides a machine-learning prediction of Antarctic geothermal heat flow and associated uncertainty products. The record states that multiple geophysical and geological predictors were used with a gradient-boosted regression-tree approach, producing a heat-flow map of about 35-156 mW/m² plus minimum, maximum and difference products. It is well suited to comparison with Aq1, Martos, GFZ lithosphere and seismological Antarctic heat-flow model families.

desc_en: CGD-UK is a Newcastle University geothermal database for the United Kingdom, structured as relational CSV files with a master table indexing individual borehole records. It compiles borehole temperature and thermal-conductivity data to support repeatable estimation of upper-crustal heat flow, and the collection description presents it as an open digital foundation for UK geothermal data. It is distinct from the UK Geothermal Platform row because it is a citable DOI dataset with structured CSV files.

desc_zh: 该 PANGAEA 记录是南非热流资料的开放 component，关联 Bullard 1939 年 South Africa 热流研究以及 Jones 1987 年 Namaqua Mobile Belt 热流与生热研究。页面给出南非范围、温度梯度、平均热导率和热流 mW/m² 字段，许可为 CC BY 3.0，并标注 122 个数据点。它是非洲南部地盾/活动带热结构研究的重要历史开放入口。
desc_en: This PANGAEA record is an open South Africa heat-flow component linked to Bullard’s 1939 heat-flow study and Jones’s 1987 work on heat flow and heat production in the Namaqua Mobile Belt. The page provides South Africa coverage, thermal gradient, average conductivity and heat flow in mW/m², with CC BY 3.0 licensing and 122 data points. It is an important historical open entry for southern African craton/mobile-belt thermal-structure studies.

notes: Accepted as a non-U.S. institutional model dataset with explicit heat-flow data and GIS formats. Access may require using the BGS NGDC accessions page rather than a single static file URL.
desc_zh: EARTH 是 British Geological Survey 发布的东非裂谷温度与热流 GIS 模型，覆盖布隆迪、吉布提、埃塞俄比亚、厄立特里亚、肯尼亚、马拉维、莫桑比克、卢旺达、坦桑尼亚、乌干达和赞比亚。BGS 元数据说明该模型由开放源数据整理为 shapefile 和 GeoTIFF 栅格，包含地热点、温泉、DEM、地表温度、地温梯度、热导率、热流、断裂、地质和人口/电网等图层。该数据在 Open Government Licence 下提供，是东非地热潜力和区域热结构研究的重要机构开放数据集。
desc_en: EARTH is a British Geological Survey GIS heat-flow and temperature model for the East African Rift, covering Burundi, Djibouti, Ethiopia, Eritrea, Kenya, Malawi, Mozambique, Rwanda, Tanzania, Uganda and Zambia. The BGS metadata states that the model was built from open-source data into shapefiles and GeoTIFF rasters, including geothermal sites, hot springs, DEM, surface temperature, geothermal gradients, thermal conductivities, heat-flow data, faults, geology, population and electrification layers. Provided under the Open Government Licence, it is an important institutional open dataset for East African geothermal potential and regional thermal-structure studies.

The page states that the ZIP archive contains Python code, input data, predicted outputs and a README for training a Random Forest model and generating a heat-flow prediction map, linked to a 2025 Geothermics manuscript. It is not a primary measurement compilation, but an open regional prediction-model package for a thermally underexplored marine rift area in Mexico.

The page lists Input_Germany.csv, Curie-depth CSVs with uncertainties, thermal-parameter CSVs, Jupyter notebooks, Python scripts and result TXT files, totalling about 20.6 MB, with CC BY 4.0 and MIT licensing. It is a useful open data/code entry point for national-scale German GHF modelling, predictor compilation and uncertainty quantification.

desc_en: The China Heat Flow Database is a public web database for Chinese terrestrial heat-flow information, with sections for heat-flow data, rock thermophysical properties, borehole temperatures and hot springs. The page provides province/region, quality, coordinate and heat-flow-value filters; popup fields include depth range, geothermal gradient, thermal conductivity, observed heat flow, corrected heat flow, quality and references, with query and export controls. It is an important institutional lead for Chinese heat-flow data, but the landing page does not clearly state an open reuse license, so exported-data redistribution should be preceded by citation and permission checks.

The GDR record states that the heat-flow model was interpolated from 66 points using kriging in ArcGIS, while the well dataset contains coordinates, temperature, quality, basement depth and heat-flow attributes. It is best treated as a site-scale FORGE heat-flow and well-data component rather than a national compilation.

time_span: publication date 2022-07-20; data time period listed as 2022-06-30
scale: regional heat-flow map and well-data support package
source_type: USGS ScienceBase data release
evidence: USGS and ScienceBase describe maps of estimated background conductive heat flow across the greater Great Basin, derived from SMU and USGS geothermal well data using iterative weighted-regression/2D LOESS methods. The release includes three maps and a point coverage for all wells, with residuals indicating departure from background heat-flow conditions.
notes: Accepted as a modern USGS regional map/support package. It overlaps conceptually with Great Basin geothermal exploration datasets, but has a distinct DOI, CC0 rights statement, downloadable ScienceBase ZIP and explicit conductive heat-flow map outputs.

desc_en: This USGS Great Basin data release provides estimated background conductive heat-flow maps and supporting well-point data for the western United States. The record states that SMU and USGS geothermal well data were processed with an iterative weighted-regression/2D LOESS workflow to produce three heat-flow maps under different measurement-quality assumptions, plus a point coverage with residuals that indicate departures from background conditions. ScienceBase provides a 124.12 MB ZIP download and the release is marked CC0 1.0.

desc_en: This USGS/ScienceBase release supports a 2023 Geothermics paper on geothermal resource favorability in the western United States. ScienceBase states that the package contains five input features, explicitly including heat flow, together with known-geothermal-system labels and nine favorability prediction maps. It is not a pure heat-flow observation table, but it provides a downloadable heat-flow feature layer and documents how heat-flow evidence enters geothermal machine-learning assessment.

The release includes GeoTIFF prediction percentile files and ScienceBase lists a 1,311,317,783 byte ZIP.

desc_en: This USGS Great Basin release supports a 2025 Geothermics paper on hydrothermal favorability mapping. The USGS page states that the data include point data for 16 input features; labeled well data use modeled departures from conductive heat flow as supervised-learning labels, and unlabeled grid features plus GeoTIFF prediction percentiles from 1,200 model realizations are provided. It is a derived heat-flow feature package rather than a raw observation table, but it is important for tracing how conductive-heat-flow departures enter modern Great Basin geothermal assessment.

The associated paper states that data and code are available at the Figshare DOI.

desc_en: This Figshare record is the data/code package for the 2024 JGR Solid Earth paper “The Distribution of Surface Heat Flow on the Tibetan Plateau Revealed by Data-Driven Methods.” The Figshare API resolves version DOI 10.6084/m9.figshare.24763149.v3, shows a CC BY 4.0 license, and lists a downloadable Heatflow_Tibetan.zip file. It focuses on data-driven prediction of Tibetan Plateau surface heat flow and analysis of controlling factors, making it a regional heat-flow modelling and reproducibility lead.

desc_zh: 该 Zenodo 记录发布了面向整个南极大陆的地热热流（GHF）网格数据与代码包，采用粒子群优化的深度神经网络生成。记录说明数据为 0.5° x 0.5° CSV 网格，单位为 mW m-2，许可为 CC BY 4.0，并提供 48.6 MB 的 Antarctica_heat_flow_pso_dnn.zip 下载。The Cryosphere 2026 论文的数据可用性声明也指向该 DOI，适合作为南极现代机器学习热流图和不确定性复现线索。
desc_en: This Zenodo record provides an Antarctic geothermal heat-flow grid and code package generated with a particle-swarm-optimized deep neural network. The record describes 0.5° x 0.5° CSV gridded GHF values in mW m-2, is licensed CC BY 4.0, and includes a 48.6 MB Antarctica_heat_flow_pso_dnn.zip download. The associated 2026 The Cryosphere paper points to the Zenodo DOI as the dataset and code used to generate its maps.

desc_zh: 该 Zenodo 记录是“基于聚类的分位数回归森林改进地热热流预测与不确定性量化”研究的开放复现实验包。记录许可为 MIT，包含 Dataset.zip、4-GHF_Data.ipynb、QRF/CPT 模型或辅助压缩包以及多个 Jupyter notebook，可用于追踪全球热流预测、不确定性量化和训练数据处理流程。它更像方法与数据复现包，而不是整理好的观测目录，因此后续接入前应检查 Dataset.zip 和 GHF_Data notebook 的具体字段。
desc_en: This Zenodo record is an open reproducibility package for a study on clustering-based quantile regression forests for geothermal heat-flow prediction and uncertainty quantification. It is licensed under MIT and includes Dataset.zip, 4-GHF_Data.ipynb, QRF/CPT support archives and several Jupyter notebooks for reproducing figures and models. It should be treated as a method/data package rather than a polished observational catalogue, with Dataset.zip and the GHF data notebook inspected before downstream extraction.

The record description states that ThermalModel_WUS.nc includes surface heat flow inputs, modeled crustal, mantle and surface heat flow, thermal conductivity, temperature and geothermal-gradient variables; it also states that ThermalModel_EastAsia.nc includes East Asia thermal model data and temperature proxies including surface heat-flow rasters.

It is not a standalone heat-flow observation database; use the NetCDF metadata to distinguish input heat-flow proxies from modeled heat-flow outputs. Earlier Zenodo DOI 10.5281/zenodo.15390785 appears to be an older related version and was deferred to avoid duplication.
desc_zh: 该 Zenodo 记录发布西美国与东亚的 3D 大陆岩石圈热结构模型及 Python 建模函数。记录说明 NetCDF 文件包含 surface heat flow 输入代理，以及模型化的 crustal、mantle 和 surface heat flow、热导率、温度和地温梯度等变量，许可为 CC BY 4.0。它适合作为区域热结构与热流场联合产品线索，但不是单纯的观测热流数据库，使用时应区分输入热流栅格和模型输出热流变量。
desc_en: This Zenodo record publishes 3D continental lithospheric thermal models for the western United States and East Asia together with a Python modelling package. The metadata states that the NetCDF files include surface heat-flow input proxies and modelled crustal, mantle and surface heat-flow variables, along with thermal conductivity, temperature and geothermal-gradient fields. It is best treated as a regional thermal-model product containing explicit heat-flow variables rather than as a standalone observational heat-flow database.

desc_zh: 该 OSF 项目公开了俯冲带沿走向地表热流连续性研究的复现实验资料。文件树包含 data/heatflow 下的 IHFC_2024_GHFDB.xlsx、HFgrid14.csv、tglobe.csv、Goutorbe 相似性表，以及多个区域子图的观测与克里金 CSV、RData 输出和摘要文件。它适合作为全球俯冲带热流插值、区域比较和方法复现线索；但 OSF 元数据未显示明确许可，实际再发布前需要核对重用权利。
desc_en: This public OSF project provides reproducibility material for a study of along-strike surface heat-flow continuity near subduction zones. Its file tree includes heat-flow inputs such as IHFC_2024_GHFDB.xlsx, HFgrid14.csv, tglobe.csv and the Goutorbe similarity table, together with regional observation and kriging CSVs, RData outputs and summaries. It is useful as a global subduction-zone heat-flow interpolation and analysis lead, but the OSF metadata did not expose an explicit license, so reuse rights should be checked before redistribution.

The README documents XLSX columns for coordinates, measurement depths, geothermal gradient, thermal conductivity, geothermal heat flow, uncertainty, references/DOIs, data-quality class, method and comments. No explicit repository license was exposed, so it is best treated as an open access discovery and citation lead pending reuse-rights checks.

desc_zh: 该 MIT 许可 GitHub 仓库支撑 Frontiers in Earth Science 2022 年“Properties and Biases of the Global Heat Flow Compilation”研究。仓库包含 IHFC_2021_GHFDB-2_obs.xlsx、obs.json 和 Jupyter notebook，用于分析全球热流观测在构造、地质、油气特征等背景中的采样偏差。它不是最新权威全球库的替代品，但对理解热流观测偏倚、辅助属性和 2021 版 IHFC 观测状态很有价值。
desc_en: This MIT-licensed GitHub repository supports the 2022 Frontiers in Earth Science study on properties and biases of the global heat-flow compilation. It includes IHFC_2021_GHFDB-2_obs.xlsx, obs.json and a Jupyter notebook for analysing how global heat-flow observations sample tectonic, geological and other contextual classes. It is not a replacement for the current GFZ/IHFC release, but it is useful for bias analysis, added attributes and the historical 2021 IHFC observation state.

desc_en: This PANGAEA dataset contains eight seafloor heat-flow measurements from R/V Vityaz Cruise 48 in the Central Pacific, including sites on the Manihiki Plateau, Marcus-Necker Rise and Hawaiian Trough. DataCite notes higher-than-average heat-flow values on submarine uplifts, and the PANGAEA landing page provides a DOI, CC BY 3.0 license and tabular download access. It is a useful citable source for historical marine heat-flow measurements.

desc_zh: 该 PANGAEA 数据集对应 R/V Akademik Sergey Vavilov 第 13 航次在 Pechora Sea 的地热测量结果表，是 Verzhbitsky 2001 年 Pechora Sea 地热研究的补充数据。PANGAEA 页面提供 DOI、CC BY 3.0 许可、事件地图元数据和下载入口。它可作为巴伦支海陆架区域海洋地热/热流观测的历史数据线索。
desc_en: This PANGAEA dataset is the supplementary table of geothermal measurements from R/V Akademik Sergey Vavilov Cruise 13 in the Pechora Sea. It accompanies Verzhbitsky's 2001 study of geothermal conditions in the region and provides DOI-backed, CC BY 3.0 tabular access through PANGAEA. It is a historical marine geothermal measurement lead for the Barents Sea shelf region.
